title: RIO Canonical Request
description: The root object that initiates the RIO decision traceability chain. Every canonical request is hashable and signable, and serves as the anchor record to which all downstream objects — authorization, execution, attestation, receipt, and ledger entry — are linked via request_id.

request_id.description: Globally unique identifier for this request. All downstream records (authorization, execution, attestation, receipt, ledger entry) reference this value to maintain an unbroken traceability chain.

requested_by.description: The entity that originated this request. May be an AI agent, automated system, or human-initiated process.

entity_id.description: Unique identifier of the requesting entity (e.g., agent ID, service account, or user ID).

display_name.description: Human-readable name of the requesting entity.

requested_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp (UTC) indicating when the request was created. Used for freshness validation and time-bound authorization checks.

action_type.description: A namespaced identifier describing the action being requested. Uses dot notation to indicate domain and operation (e.g., 'finance.send_payment', 'infrastructure.delete_resource', 'communications.send_email').

target.description: The resource or system that the requested action will affect.

target_id.description: Unique identifier of the target resource.

target_label.description: Human-readable label for the target resource.

parameters.description: Action-specific parameters. The structure varies by action_type. These parameters are included in the canonical hash to ensure that any modification to the requested action is detectable.

business_reason.description: The justification for this request, as provided or inferred by the requesting entity. Preserved for audit and human review.

summary.description: Brief human-readable explanation of why this action is being requested.

supporting_references.description: Optional references to supporting evidence (e.g., invoice IDs, ticket numbers, policy citations).

reference_id.description: Identifier or locator for the reference.

risk_context.description: Risk assessment metadata attached at intake. Used by the policy engine and human reviewers to evaluate the request.

risk_level.description: Assessed risk level of the requested action.

risk_factors.description: Specific factors contributing to the risk assessment.

amount.description: Estimated monetary value of the action.

reversibility.description: Whether the action can be undone after execution.

policy_context.description: Policy evaluation metadata. Identifies which policies apply to this request and what constraints they impose.

applicable_policies.description: List of policy identifiers that govern this request.

version.description: Version of the policy being applied.

requires_authorization.description: Whether this request requires explicit human authorization before execution.

authorization_type.description: The type of authorization workflow required, as determined by policy evaluation.

constraints.description: Specific constraints imposed by applicable policies.

value.description: The constraint value. Type varies by constraint_type.
